AUTHOR: Rozenweig, S.
TOPIC: Interactive Signal Processing Tools for Analyzing Multitrack Singing Voice Recordings
METHODOLOGY:
- Computational tools from signal processing, computer science, and music information retrieval (MIR)
- Time-scale modification (TSM) and resampling techniques for time-varying pitch shifts
- Fusing outputs of several F0-estimation algorithms for reliability assessment
- Musically motivated filtering techniques for stable region detection and pitch drift compensation
- Interactive feedback mechanisms for incorporating musical knowledge
- Morphological operations and binary time-frequency masks for removing pitch slides and frequency fluctuations
DATASET:
- Dagstuhl ChoirSet: Western choral music, focusing on intonation control.
- Erkomaishvili dataset: Traditional Georgian singing, focusing on pitch slides and harmonic intervals.
- Zär corpus: Traditional Georgian funeral songs, focusing on pitch drift correction and harmonic interval analysis.
APA FORMAT: Rosenzweig, S. (2022). Interactive Signal Processing Tools for Analyzing Multitrack Singing Voice Recordings [Doctoral dissertation, Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg]. University Library of Erlangen-Nuremberg.

AUTHOR: Gu, X., Ou, L., Zeng, W., Zhang, J., Wong, N., & Wang, Y.
TOPIC: Automatic Lyric Transcription and Automatic Music Transcription from Multimodal Singing
METHODOLOGY:
- General framework for multimodal ALT and AMT systems
- Adaptation of self-supervised learning (SSL) models from speech domain
- Residual cross-attention mechanism for feature integration
- Sequence-to-sequence (S2S) problems for ALT and AMT
- Hybrid CTC-Attention for ALT
- Frame-level classification for AMT
- Two-stage training approach
- Evaluation techniques including ablation studies
DATASET: N20EMv1, N20EMv2 (multimodal singing datasets), DSing, MIR-ST500, TONAS, ISMIR2014 (benchmark datasets).
APA FORMAT: Gu, X., Ou, L., Zeng, W., Zhang, J., Wong, N., & Wang, Y. (2024). Automatic Lyric Transcription and Automatic Music Transcription from Multimodal Singing. ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications, 20(7), Article 209. https://doi.org/10.1145/3651310

AUTHOR: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., Petermann, D., & Gómez, E.
TOPIC: A Deep-Learning Based Framework for Source Separation, Analysis, and Synthesis of Choral Ensembles
METHODOLOGY:
- Use of publicly available choral singing datasets for training and evaluation.
- Evaluation of monophonic F0 estimators and proposal of an approximation for perceived F0.
- Synthesis of a single singer voice from unison and transposing/remixing separated stems.
- Listening tests for perceptual evaluation.
- Use of BSS eval metrics for objective evaluation.
- Selection and comparison of deep learning models (Open-Unmix, Conv-TasNet, U-Net, Wave-U-Net).
- Use of pYIN and CREPE for F0 estimation.
DATASET:
- Choral Singing Dataset (CSD), Dagstuhl ChoirSet (DCS), ESMUC Choir Dataset (ECD), Bach Chorales Dataset (BCD).
- Access: CSD and DCS available at https://zenodo.org/
APA FORMAT: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., Petermann, D., & Gómez, E. (2022). A Deep-Learning Based Framework for Source Separation, Analysis, and Synthesis of Choral Ensembles. Frontiers in Signal Processing, 2, Article 808594. https://doi.org/10.3389/frsip.2022.808594

AUTHOR: Cairns, P., Daffern, H., & Kearney, G.
TOPIC: Parametric Evaluation of Ensemble Vocal Performance Using an Immersive Network Music Performance Audio System
METHODOLOGY:
- Prototype implementation of the VIIVA-NMP audio system using open-source tools
- JackTrip for audio streaming with UDP, jitter buffering, and FEC
- Auralization using convolution with Ambisonic impulse responses and binaural decoding
- Jack Audio Connection Kit for routing audio
- TIMEX-Lite onset detection method implemented in MATLAB
- Two-way repeated measures ANOVA for statistical analysis
DATASET: Performance data from ten singers across Europe, organized into five duet pairs, tested in three virtual acoustic environments (recording studio booth, medium hall, cathedral).
APA FORMAT: Cairns, P., Daffern, H., & Kearney, G. (2021). Parametric Evaluation of Ensemble Vocal Performance Using an Immersive Network Music Performance Audio System. Journal of the Audio Engineering Society, 69(12), 924–933. https://doi.org/10.17743/jaes.2021.0040

AUTHOR: Chandna, P.
TOPIC: Neural Networks For Singing Voice
METHODOLOGY:
- Non-autoregressive WaveNet architecture for spectral envelope synthesis parameters and f0 estimation
- State-of-the-art source separation algorithms for individual voice separation
- Mask-based source separation algorithms for mixture recordings
- Feedforward U-Net based architecture for compressed spectral envelope generation
- NUS corpus for training
- Comparison with NPSS singing voice synthesizer
- WORLD vocoder with reduced dimensions for synthesis
- Deep learning algorithms for SATB choral part separation
- CREPE system for single voice signal synthesis from unison content
- mir_eval library for evaluation with 30 cents pitch tolerance
DATASET: Choral Singing Dataset (CSD), Bach chorales dataset (BCD), ESMUC dataset, Daghstuhl dataset (DSD)
APA FORMAT: Chandna, P. (2021). Neural Networks For Singing Voice Extraction In Monaural Polyphonic Music Signals [Doctoral dissertation, Universitat Pompeu Fabra]. UPF e-Repositori.

AUTHOR: Sarkar, S., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M.
TOPIC: EnsembleSet: A new high quality synthesised dataset for chamber ensemble separation.
METHODOLOGY:
- Data generation using Spitfire BBC Symphony Orchestra library and ensemble scores from RWC Classical Music Database and Mutopia.
- Training a permutation-invariant time-domain separation model using the Dual-path Transformer (DPTNet) architecture.
- Use of PIT (permutation-invariant training) for source separation.
- Data augmentation techniques: gain modulation, channel swap, pitchshifting.
- Training on NVIDIA A100 GPUs using a distributed data parallel back-end.
- Evaluation using metrics like SDR and SI-SDR.
DATASET:
- EnsembleSet.
- Access: Available on Zenodo.
APA FORMAT: Sarkar, S., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M. (2022). EnsembleSet: A new high quality synthesised dataset for chamber ensemble separation. In Proceedings of the 23rd International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 625–632). International Society for Music Information Retrieval.

AUTHOR: Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E.
TOPIC: Voice Assignment in Vocal Quartets Using Deep Learning Models Based on Pitch Salience
METHODOLOGY:
- Two-stage pipeline combining multiple F0 estimation with voice assignment using deep learning.
- Two novel architectures: VoasCNN (fully convolutional) and VoasCLSTM (convolutional LSTM).
- Training using cross-entropy loss minimization.
- Evaluation using F-Score from mir_eval library.
- Post-processing includes locating maximum salience bins and thresholding.
DATASET:
- Synth-salience Choral Set (SSCS), Barbershop Quartets Dataset (BSQ), Cantoría dataset.
- Dataset access: SSCS and Cantoría dataset are hosted on Zenodo.
APA FORMAT: Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E. (2022). Voice Assignment in Vocal Quartets Using Deep Learning Models Based on Pitch Salience. Transactions of the International Society for Music Information Retrieval, 5(1), 99–112. https://doi.org/10.5334/tismir.121

AUTHOR: Weiß, C., Henzel, B., & Müller, M.
TOPIC: Style Evolution in Western Choral Music: A Corpus-Based Strategy.
METHODOLOGY:
- Computing evolution curves to map musical complexity onto historical timelines
- Use of audio recordings as primary data source
- Deep learning (DL) for pitch-class activation estimation
- Deep convolutional neural network (ResNet) for feature extraction
- Heuristic method for estimating missing work dates based on composers' life spans
- Geometric complexity measure for tonal complexity
- Validation and optimization of parameters using reference curves
- Mixed approach for computing evolution curves using DL and SP features
- Systematic comparison of SP vs. DL feature types
DATASET:
- Carus Audio Corpus (CAC) - 5,700 tracks, 450 years of sacred and choral music.
- Access: Not publicly available due to commercial restrictions; detailed information and preview files available on the Carus-Verlag website.
APA FORMAT: Weiß, C., Henzel, B., & Müller, M. (2025). Style Evolution in Western Choral Music: A Corpus-Based Strategy. Computational Humanities Research. https://doi.org/10.1017/chr.2025.10016

AUTHOR: Sarkar, S.
TOPIC: Time-domain Music Source Separation for Choirs and Ensembles
METHODOLOGY:
- Deep learning techniques using time-domain models with permutation invariant training (PIT)
- Use of EnsembleSet, a high-quality synthesised dataset for chamber ensemble music
- Fine-tuning for cross-dataset performance improvement
- Optimization of TasNets for high sampling rates and study of hyperparameters
- Novel measure for harmonic complexity
- SI-SDR as loss function
- Data augmentation using torch-audiomentations
- Training on NVIDIA A100 GPUs with distributed data parallel backend
DATASET:
- EnsembleSet, URMP
- Access: EnsembleSet available on Zenodo
APA FORMAT: Sarkar, S. (2024). Time-domain Music Source Separation for Choirs and Ensembles [Doctoral dissertation, Queen Mary University of London]. Queen Mary Research Online.

AUTHOR: Schulze-Forster, K., Richard, G., Kelley, L., Doire, C. S. J., & Badeau, R.
TOPIC: Unsupervised Music Source Separation Using Differentiable Parametric Source Models
METHODOLOGY:
- Unsupervised model-based deep learning approach for musical source separation
- Differentiable parametric source-filter model for each source
- Neural network trained to reconstruct mixture by estimating source parameters based on fundamental frequencies
- Deep learning architecture similar to previous work
- Evaluation using Scale-Invariant Source-to-Distortion ratio (SI-SDR)
- Implementation using PyTorch framework
- Re-implementation and extension of Algorithm 1 and all-pole filter
DATASET: Bach Chorals (BC) dataset, Barbershop Quartet (BQ) dataset, Choral Singing Dataset.
APA FORMAT: Schulze-Forster, K., Richard, G., Kelley, L., Doire, C. S. J., & Badeau, R. (2023). Unsupervised Music Source Separation Using Differentiable Parametric Source Models. IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 31, 1276–1289. https://doi.org/10.1109/TASLP.2023.3252272

AUTHOR: Chadabe, J.
TOPIC: The history of electronic music as a reflection of structural paradigms
METHODOLOGY: The author proposes that new and similar paradigms appear in all fields simultaneously. He posits that the two most important developments in the history of electronic music were (1) the opening up of music to all sounds and (2) the development of interactive instruments. He discusses the first development at length, suggesting ways in which it reflected general paradigm shifts in other fields. He then points to ways in which the second development may also reflect broad paradigm shifts, and concludes by speculating about the direction that such paradigmatic changes may be taking in our time.
APA FORMAT: Chadabe, J. (1996). The history of electronic music as a reflection of structural paradigms. Leonardo Music Journal, 6, 41. https://doi.org/10.2307/1513303

AUTHOR: Batchelor, C., Gooding, J., Marriott, W., Chalashkanov, N., Tucker, N., & Margetts, R.
TOPIC: Musical Instruments / Types (Guitar)
METHODOLOGY:
- Creation of dedicated experimental pickups to isolate variables
- Measurement of impedance using a frequency response analyzer
- Analysis of resonant peak and Q-factor variations based on winding count and wire gauge
DATASET: - Measurements from custom-wound single-coil Stratocaster-style pickups (varying 5000-12000 turns, 42/44 AWG wire).
APA FORMAT: Batchelor, C., Gooding, J., Marriott, W., Chalashkanov, N., Tucker, N., & Margetts, R. (2024). Guitar Pickups I: Analysis of the effect of winding and wire gauge on single coil electric guitar pickups. arXiv (Cornell University). https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2409.19782

AUTHOR: Lee, S. W., Heo, J. H., Lee, S. T., & Gim, G. Y.
TOPIC: Musical Instruments / Types (Drums)
METHODOLOGY:
- Feature extraction using Mel Spectrograms
- Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for classification
- Comparison of hyper-parameters for recognition rates
DATASET: - IDMT-SMT-Drums data set (acoustic and electronic drum recordings).
APA FORMAT: Lee, S. W., Heo, J. H., Lee, S. T., & Gim, G. Y. (2021). A study on drum transcription using machine learning focused on discrimination of acoustic drum and electronic drum sound. In Studies in computational intelligence (pp. 141–153). https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-67008-5_12

AUTHOR: Lisena, P., Meroño-Peñuela, A., & Troncy, R.
TOPIC: Technology / Control Standards
METHODOLOGY:
- Graph embedding technique (MIDI2vec) adapted from node2vec
- Representation of MIDI data as graphs (nodes for tempo, programs, notes)
- Random walks on MIDI graphs to generate vector embeddings
DATASET: - Large corpus of MIDI files converted into graph representations
APA FORMAT: Lisena, P., Meroño-Peñuela, A., & Troncy, R. (2021). MIDI2vec: Learning MIDI embeddings for reliable prediction of symbolic music metadata. Semantic Web, 13(3), 357–377. https://doi.org/10.3233/sw-210446

AUTHOR: Worrall, K., Yin, Z., & Collins, T.
TOPIC: Technology / Control Standards
METHODOLOGY:
- Development of Cue-Free Express + Pedal (CFE+P) transformer model
- "In the wild" comparative evaluation against professional software (Logic Pro, Ableton)
- Subjective listening tests to rate expressivity
DATASET: - Results from listening studies comparing model outputs vs. human/commercial baselines.
APA FORMAT: Worrall, K., Yin, Z., & Collins, T. (2024). Comparative evaluation in the wild: Systems for the expressive rendering of music. IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence, 1–15. https://doi.org/10.1109/tai.2024.3408717

AUTHOR: Piao, Z., Frisson, C., Van Kerrebroeck, B., & Wanderley, M. M.
TOPIC: Technology / Sensors & Haptics
METHODOLOGY:
- User study with 20 musically trained participants
- Evaluation of 3 haptic modes (No-FF, Spring, Detent)
- Vibrato mimicry tasks and Likert-scale user feedback
DATASET: - User performance metrics (Pitch Error, Pitch Deviation) and subjective experience ratings.
APA FORMAT: Piao, Z., Frisson, C., Van Kerrebroeck, B., & Wanderley, M. M. (2024). Assessing the impact of force feedback in musical knobs on performance and user experience. Actuators, 13(11), 462. https://doi.org/10.3390/act13110462

AUTHOR: Lee, J. W., Park, J., Choi, M. J., & Lee, K.
TOPIC: Technology / Sensors & Haptics
METHODOLOGY:
- Differentiable Modal Synthesis (DMSP) framework
- Neural network solving partial differential equations (PDE) for nonlinear strings
- Joint optimization of sound and motion simulation
DATASET: - Synthesized string sounds and motion data used for ground-truth loss calculation.
APA FORMAT: Lee, J. W., Park, J., Choi, M. J., & Lee, K. (2024). Differentiable modal synthesis for physical modeling of planar string sound and motion simulation. arXiv (Cornell University). https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2407.05516

AUTHOR: Tsoi, T., Deng, J., Ju, Y., Weck, B., Kirchhoff, H., & Lui, S.
TOPIC: Cross-modal music similarity retrieval using contrastive learning, guided by LLM-generated text descriptions.
METHODOLOGY:
- The system uses a cross-modal contrastive learning framework with separate music and text encoders projected into a shared embedding space. 
- It features a dual-source data acquisition method combining online data mining with an LLM-based prompting methodology to generate diverse text descriptions for songs.
DATASET:
- A new, large-scale dataset of music-text pairs was created using the proposed dual-source acquisition method. 
- The framework was validated through objective metrics, subjective user evaluations, and online A/B testing on the Huawei Music streaming platform.
APA FORMAT: Tsoi, T., Deng, J., Ju, Y., Weck, B., Kirchhoff, H., & Lui, S. (2025). CrossMuSim: A Cross-Modal Framework for Music Similarity Retrieval with LLM-Powered Text Description Sourcing and Mining. In Proceedings of the IEEE International Conference on Multimedia and Expo (ICME).

AUTHOR: Zhou, Z., Wu, Y., Wu, Z., Zhang, X., Yuan, R., Ma, Y., Wang, L., Benetos, E., Xue, W., & Guo, Y.
TOPIC: A systematic evaluation of the music understanding, generation, and multi-step reasoning capabilities of state-of-the-art Large Language Models.
METHODOLOGY:
- The research introduces ABC-Eval, a novel open-source benchmark designed to evaluate the instruction-following and understanding capabilities of LLMs on symbolic music represented in ABC notation. 
- Seven leading LLMs were evaluated on this benchmark.
DATASET: - The newly created ABC-Eval benchmark, an open-source dataset of tasks based on ABC notation scores designed to test musical reasoning.
APA FORMAT: Zhou, Z., Wu, Y., Wu, Z., Zhang, X., Yuan, R., Ma, Y., Wang, L., Benetos, E., Xue, W., & Guo, Y. (2024). Can LLMs “Reason” in Music? an Evaluation of LLMs’ Capability of Music Understanding and Generation. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR).

AUTHOR: Wu, S., Yu, D., Tan, X., & Sun, M.
TOPIC: A pre-trained model, CLaMP, that learns joint representations of natural language and symbolic music for cross-modal retrieval tasks, including semantic search and zero-shot classification.
METHODOLOGY:
- CLaMP utilizes a dual-encoder architecture trained with a contrastive loss. 
- Key innovations include "bar patching" for efficient sequence representation, text dropout as data augmentation, and a masked music model pre-training objective to enhance contextual understanding.
DATASET: - The model was pre-trained on a new dataset of 1.4 million music-text pairs. For evaluation, the authors created and released the WikiMusicText (WikiMT) dataset, containing 1,010 lead sheets in ABC notation with descriptive annotations.
APA FORMAT: Wu, S., Yu, D., Tan, X., & Sun, M. (2023). CLaMP: Contrastive Language-Music Pre-Training for Cross-Modal Symbolic Music Information Retrieval. In Proceedings of the 24th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 157-165).

AUTHOR: Yuan, R., Pan, J., Li, Y., Zhang, G., Zhang, Y., Duan, Z., Xue, W., & Guo, Y.
TOPIC: A robust music version identification system, X-Cover, that improves upon the state-of-the-art by integrating features from a pre-trained ASR model to leverage lyrical content
METHODOLOGY:
- X-Cover extends the ByteCover3 architecture with a dual-input structure, processing a CQT spectrogram for musical features and a log-Mel spectrogram via a parameter-efficiently fine-tuned Whisper model for lyrical features. 
- The embeddings are fused and trained using a Local Alignment Loss (LAL) and a "bag of tricks" including Sparse Softmax Loss and Non-Local operations.
DATASET: - The system was evaluated on two standard Cover Song Identification benchmarks: SHS100K, a large-scale dataset with 108,523 recordings, and Covers80, a smaller classic test set.
APA FORMAT: Yuan, R., Pan, J., Li, Y., Zhang, G., Zhang, Y., Duan, Z., Xue, W., & Guo, Y. (2024). X-Cover: Better Music Version Identification System by Integrating Pretrained ASR Model. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 70-77).

AUTHOR: Xu, S., Yu, C., Li, Y., & Li, H.
TOPIC: Paralinguistic singing attribute recognition for classical tenor voice using supervised machine learning frameworks
METHODOLOGY:
- Constructed Singing Voice Quality and Technique Database (SVQTD) with 4,000 labeled tenor vocal segments from YouTube
- Music source separation (Spleeter) and energy-based silence detection used for preprocessing
- Seven trained annotators labeled data with seven paralinguistic singing attributes after standardized training
- Explored three supervised machine learning frameworks for attribute recognition:
- SF-SVM: openSMILE handcrafted features (INTERSPEECH 2009/2016) + SVM classifier
- E2EDL: End-to-end deep learning using ResNet and Transformer Encoder on log-mel spectrograms
- DE-SVM: Deep embeddings from E2EDL fed into SVM classifier
- Introduced Sliced Multi-Head Self-Attention (SMSA) for efficient 2D spectrogram processing in transformers
- Addressed class imbalance with weighted cross-entropy and focal loss
- Evaluated models using Unweighted Average Recall (UAR) metric
DATASET:
- Name: Singing Voice Quality and Technique Database (SVQTD)
- Content: 4000 vocal solo segments (4–20 seconds each)
- Source: YouTube recordings of six famous tenor arias
- Annotations: Labeled with seven paralinguistic singing attributes (e.g., chest resonance, head resonance, open throat, vibrato, etc.)
- Annotators: 7 trained classical singers (≥3 years experience)
- Labeling Process: 10-hour training (2 hours theory + 8 hours practice); supervised quality checks
Availability: Publicly available online — https://hackerpeter1.github.io/SVQTD/
APA FORMAT: Xu, S., Yu, C., Li, Y., & Li, H. (2022). Paralinguistic singing attribute recognition for classical tenor voice using supervised machine learning frameworks. EURASIP Journal on Audio, Speech, and Music Processing, 2022(8). https://doi.org/10.1186/s13636-022-00240-z

AUTHOR: Huang, D.
TOPIC: An objective evaluation method of vocal singing effect based on artificial intelligence technology
METHODOLOGY:
- Logarithmic Mel spectrum extracted as main acoustic feature for accurate voice characterization.
- Preprocessing with resampling, framing, and windowing (Hamming window) for spectral analysis.
- Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) used to convert framed signals to frequency domain.
- Hidden Markov Models (HMM) applied for acoustic modeling and phoneme recognition.
- Hybrid Gaussian models combined with HMM to model emission probabilities of acoustic states.
- Generalized Regression Radial Basis Function (RBF) Network used for objective evaluation scoring.
- Evaluation criteria included 18 performance indicators (intonation, technique, emotion, stage presence).
- System accuracy: 95% (pitch), 93% (rhythm), 89% (timbre).
- Validation: Compared system-generated scores with human expert judgments to confirm reliability.
DATASET:
- Focused on solo vocal singing audio recordings (clean monophonic tracks).
- Audio sampled at 42.2 kHz, 16-bit mono format.
- Data used for pitch, rhythm, and timbre analysis in vocal performance evaluation.
- Songs such as “Water of Forgetfulness” and “Courage” were used for testing system reliability.
- Evaluations included multiple singers (male/female) and human expert comparisons for validation.
APA FORMAT: Huang, D. (2024). An objective evaluation method of vocal singing effect based on artificial intelligence technology. Applied Mathematics and Nonlinear Sciences, 9(1), 1–13. https://doi.org/10.2478/amns-2024-0865

AUTHOR: Angelakis, E., Kotsani, N., & Georgaki, A.
TOPIC: Towards a Singing Voice Multi-Sensor Analysis Tool: System Design, and Assessment Based on Vocal Breathiness
METHODOLOGY:
Designed a modular multi-sensor system specifically for singing voice analysis.
- Condenser Microphone (CM): To capture the acoustic vocal output.
- Electroglottograph (EGG): To monitor phonatory function and vocal fold contact.
- Respiratory Effort Transducers (RET): Two belts (thoracic and abdominal) to track respiratory behavior.
Methodological focus on assessing "vocal breathiness" through:
- Perceptual ratings of breathiness in recorded samples.
- Feature extraction from CM and EGG signals, specifically Smoothed Cepstral Peak Prominence (CPPS) and Open Quotient (OQ) using Howard (HOQ) and DECOM (DOQ) methods.
- Conducted correlation analysis between perceptual ratings and extracted sensor features.
Proposed a multi-variate regression model (CDH) combining CPPS, DOQ, and HOQ, comparing it against the Acoustic Breathiness Index (ABI).
DATASET:
The study utilizes a custom experimental dataset created specifically for this system assessment. Dataset details:

Participants: 2 professional singers.

Protocol: Singers performed a protocol of vocal trials producing various degrees of intended vocal breathiness.

Data: Synchronized recordings of acoustic audio, electroglottographic signals, and respiratory movement data.
APA FORMAT: Angelakis, E., Kotsani, N., & Georgaki, A. (2021). Towards a Singing Voice Multi-Sensor Analysis Tool: System Design, and Assessment Based on Vocal Breathiness. Sensors, 21(23), 8006. https://doi.org/10.3390/s21238006

AUTHOR: Heydari, M., Wang, J.-C., & Duan, Z.
TOPIC: SingNet: a real-time Singing Voice beat and Downbeat Tracking System
METHODOLOGY:
- A real-time, causal beat and downbeat tracking system designed specifically for isolated singing voices
- Utilizes a causal Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network (CRNN) with three unidirectional LSTM layers to model beat, downbeat, and non-beat activations from spectral features
- Introduces a novel dynamic particle filtering inference framework with a variable number of particles, extending traditional fixed-particle Monte Carlo methods
- Employs a two-stage particle filtering cascade for joint beat and downbeat tracking under real-time causality constraints
- Evaluated using F1-score metrics under multiple temporal tolerances (±70 ms and ±200 ms) and cross-dataset testing
DATASET:
GTZAN dataset (vocal stems): Public music dataset where vocal tracks were extracted using music source separation to enable singing voice beat and downbeat evaluation

In-house singing voice dataset: Approximately 45,000 clips of clean vocal stems and corresponding accompaniments with beat and downbeat annotations, split into training, validation, and test sets

Cross-dataset evaluation performed by training on the in-house dataset and testing on GTZAN to assess generalization
APA FORMAT: Heydari, M., Wang, J.-C., & Duan, Z. (2023). SingNet: A real-time singing voice beat and downbeat tracking system. In Proceedings of the 2023 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP) (pp. 1–5). IEEE. https://doi.org/10.1109/ICASSP49357.2023.10096580

AUTHOR: Bienhold, J. G., Lewellen, R., Meyer, D., & van Leer, E.
TOPIC: Acoustic Analysis of Blues, Country, Folk, Italian Opera, and Rock Singing
METHODOLOGY:
- Applies nonlinear dynamic acoustic measures to quantify vocal chaos across different singing genres
- Computes cepstral peak prominence (CPP), correlation dimension (D2), and vocal tract chaos parameters (VTCP) from sustained sung vowels
- Uses FFT-based spectral analysis and nonlinear time-series methods to characterize phonatory and stylistic differences
- Performs comparative statistical analysis to examine genre-dependent acoustic behavior and vocal function variability
DATASET:
Singing voice recordings from performers across multiple musical genres

Sustained vowel samples used for controlled acoustic analysis

Dataset curated specifically for the study; recordings not publicly released
APA FORMAT: Bienhold, J. G., Lewellen, R., Meyer, D., & van Leer, E. (2025). Nonlinear acoustic analysis of vocal chaos across singing genres. Journal of Voice. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jvoice.2025.02.036

AUTHOR: Baker, C. P., Purdy, S. C., Rakena, T. O., & Bonnini, S.
TOPIC: It Sounds like It Feels: Preliminary Exploration of an Aeroacoustic Diagnostic Protocol for Singers
METHODOLOGY:
- Explores associations between singers’ self-perceived vocal fatigue and instrumental acoustic and aerodynamic measures
- Computes smoothed cepstral peak prominence (CPPS), vibrato jitter, vibrato shimmer, and an efficiency ratio derived from aerodynamic measurements
- Applies multivariate linear regression to construct a proof-of-concept aeroacoustic index related to vocal fatigue perception
- Evaluates relationships between instrumental measures and selected items from the Evaluation of Ability to Sing Easily (EASE) scale
DATASET:
Acoustic and aerodynamic recordings from 18 classically trained singers

Sustained sung vowel and repeated syllable tasks recorded in a controlled environment

Dataset collected for the study; participant recordings not publicly released
APA FORMAT: Baker, C. P., Purdy, S. C., Rakena, T. O., & Bonnini, S. (2023). It sounds like it feels: Preliminary exploration of an aeroacoustic diagnostic protocol for singers. Journal of Clinical Medicine, 12(15), 5130. https://doi.org/10.3390/jcm12155130

AUTHOR: de Souza, G. V. S., Duarte, J. M. T., Viegas, F., Simões-Zenari, M., & Nemr, K.
TOPIC: An Acoustic Examination of Pitch Variation in Soprano Singing
METHODOLOGY:
- Conducts controlled acoustic analysis of sustained soprano vowel phonation across low, medium, and high pitch conditions
- Extracts fundamental frequency and the first five formants using FFT and Linear Predictive Coding (LPC) techniques
- Compares formant–harmonic relationships across pitch registers to identify pitch-dependent vocal tract behavior
- Applies non-parametric statistical tests to assess systematic differences in acoustic measures across pitch conditions
DATASET:
Recordings from 30 female sopranos without reported vocal complaints

Sustained /a/ vowel samples recorded at three pitch levels (C4, Eb4, Bb4)

Total of 90 analyzed samples; dataset recorded specifically for the study
APA FORMAT: de Souza, G. V. S., Duarte, J. M. T., Viegas, F., Simões-Zenari, M., & Nemr, K. (2020). An acoustic examination of pitch variation in soprano singing. Journal of Voice, 34(6), 925.e1–925.e10. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jvoice.2019.08.013

AUTHOR: Morelli, M. S., Orlandi, S., & Manfredi, C.
TOPIC: BioVoice: A multipurpose tool for voice analysis
METHODOLOGY:
- Introduces BioVoice, a user-friendly software platform for acoustic analysis of human voice across newborn, child, adult, and singing voice emissions
- Implements time- and frequency-domain signal processing techniques to extract fundamental frequency (F0), formant frequencies (F1–F5), jitter, noise level (NNE), vibrato rate and extent, and quality ratio (QR)
- Employs adaptive analysis strategies based on age range, gender, and vocal emission type to ensure robustness across low- and high-pitched voices
- Includes automatic detection of voiced/unvoiced segments and melodic shape analysis of F0 for newborn, child, and singing voice signals
- Provides comparative evaluation against Praat using statistical analyses (e.g., Mann–Whitney tests) to assess accuracy and reliability of extracted acoustic parameters
DATASET:
Real voice recordings from multiple categories: adult male, adult female, child, newborn, and singer

Includes sustained vowels, crying sounds, and professional singing voice samples

Supplemented with synthetic voice signals for validation of F0, formant, jitter, and noise estimation algorithms

Datasets collected specifically for evaluation and demonstration purposes; not publicly released as a standalone corpus
APA FORMAT: Morelli, M. S., Orlandi, S., & Manfredi, C. (2021). BioVoice: A multipurpose tool for voice analysis. Biomedical Signal Processing and Control, 64, 102302. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bspc.2020.102302

AUTHOR: Baker, C. P., Sundberg, J., Purdy, S. C., Rakena, T. O., & de S Leao, S. H.
TOPIC: CPPS and Voice-Source Parameters: Objective Analysis of the Singing Voice
METHODOLOGY:
- Conducts a controlled analysis of how voice-source parameters influence smoothed cepstral peak prominence (CPPS) in singing voice signals
- Uses synthetically generated singing tones to independently vary fundamental frequency, vibrato extent, source-spectrum tilt, and amplitude of the voice-source fundamental
- Generates signals using the Madde formant synthesizer under a linear source–filter model
- Computes CPPS using PRAAT and applies statistical correlation and regression analyses to quantify parameter effects
- Identifies non-linear and f₀-dependent behavior of CPPS and proposes correction equations as a proof-of-concept
DATASET:
Synthetic singing voice signals comprising 129 tones generated on vowels /a/ and /i/

Pitch range spanning 87–699 Hz, covering Western classical singing tessitura

Controlled variation of vibrato extent, source-spectrum tilt, and voice-source parameters

Dataset generated specifically for the study; not publicly released
APA FORMAT: Baker, C. P., Sundberg, J., Purdy, S. C., Rakena, T. O., & de S Leao, S. H. (2021). CPPS and voice-source parameters: Objective analysis of the singing voice. Journal of Voice, 38(3), 549–560. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jvoice.2021.12.010

AUTHOR: Truong, T., Arzt, A., & Widmer, G.
TOPIC: Deep Learning Approaches in Topics of Singing Information Processing
METHODOLOGY:
- Survey-based analysis of deep learning models applied to singing information processing.
- Identified five core SIP tasks:
Singing Voice Separation
Singing Voice Detection
Singing Voice Pitch Extraction
Singing Synthesis
Singing Voice Conversion
- Analyzed model architectures used in these tasks:
- Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for spectral feature learning.
- Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs/LSTMs) for temporal sequence modeling.
- Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Autoencoders for synthesis and timbre modeling.
- Transformer architectures for attention-based vocal processing.
- Highlighted advancements in end-to-end systems reducing reliance on hand-crafted features.
- Evaluated challenges such as data scarcity, generalization across singers, and interpretability.
DATASET:
- The study references multiple datasets related to singing voice tasks rather than one specific dataset.
- Datasets include:
- MIR-1K: 1,000 song clips for singing voice separation.
- NUS-48E: 48 English songs by 12 singers for pitch extraction and synthesis.
- JAMENDO and DSD100: Used for source separation and vocal detection tasks.
- These datasets cover monophonic and polyphonic recordings to evaluate deep learning systems for singing information processing (SIP).
APA FORMAT: Truong, T., Arzt, A., & Widmer, G. (2021). Deep learning approaches in topics of singing information processing. Frontiers in Digital Humanities, 8, 1–18. https://doi.org/10.3389/fdigh.2021.671430

AUTHOR: Li, W., Cui, X., Manoharan, P., Dai, L., Liu, K., & Huang, L.
TOPIC: AI-Assisted Feedback and Reflection in Vocal Music Training: Effects on Metacognition and Singing Performance
METHODOLOGY: The study employed an experimental design with a control group (conventional instruction) and an experimental group (AI-assisted intervention) over a six-week training period. The experimental intervention included self-assessment via audio comparison tools and dialogic feedback through interaction with a Large Language Model (AI chatbot) tailored for vocal pedagogy. The researchers measured outcomes using pre- and post-tests for singing performance and metacognitive scales.
DATASET: The dataset consisted of 80 pre-service teachers (university students). Baseline measurements included the Stanford-Binet Intelligence Scales and the Seashore Measures of Musical Talents to ensure comparable groups.
APA FORMAT: Li, W., Cui, X., Manoharan, P., Dai, L., Liu, K., & Huang, L. (2025). AI-assisted feedback and reflection in vocal music training: Effects on metacognition and singing performance. Frontiers in Psychology, 16, 1598867. https://doi.org/10.3389/fpsyg.2025.1598867

AUTHOR: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E.
TOPIC: A Deep Learning Based Analysis-Synthesis Framework for Unison Singing
METHODOLOGY: The authors developed a deep learning framework to analyze the Fundamental Frequency (F0) distribution of individual singers within a unison mixture. They proposed a system that synthesizes a choral unison signal by taking an a cappella input (a single voice prototype) and generating the necessary pitch and timing deviations found in real choirs. Subjective listening tests were used to evaluate the perceptual quality and "choir-like" nature of the synthesis.
DATASET: The study utilized multitrack choral recordings where individual singers were recorded separately to allow for precise analysis of F0 distributions in unison sections. This likely includes data from the Choral Singing Dataset (CSD) and other private multitrack collections.
APA FORMAT: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E. (2020). A deep learning based analysis-synthesis framework for unison singing. Proceedings of the 21st International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 598–604). Montreal, Canada. https://archives.ismir.net/ismir2020/paper/000259.pdf

AUTHOR: Jeon, C.-B., Moon, H., Choi, K., Chon, B. S., & Lee, K.
TOPIC: MedleyVox: An Evaluation Dataset for Multiple Singing Voices Separation
METHODOLOGY: The authors define the problem by categorizing multi-singing tasks and propose a construction strategy to create training mixtures from single-singing datasets. They introduce the Improved Super-Resolution Network (iSRNet), which is jointly trained with Conv-TasNet to enhance separation performance.
DATASET: MedleyVox (a curated evaluation dataset for multiple singing voices) and a constructed training set using various single-voice corpora.
APA FORMAT: Jeon, C.-B., Moon, H., Choi, K., Chon, B. S., & Lee, K. (2023). MedleyVox: An evaluation dataset for multiple singing voices separation. 2023 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP), 1–5. https://doi.org/10.1109/ICASSP49357.2023.10095425

AUTHOR: Mróz, B., Odya, P., & Kostek, B.
TOPIC: Creating a Remote Choir Performance Recording Based on an Ambisonic Approach
METHODOLOGY: The researchers utilized Ambisonic microphone techniques (specifically a first-order Ambisonic microphone) to record individual singers and then processed the audio to simulate a choir performance. They also employed eye-tracking technology to analyze the visual engagement of the conductor/listener with the virtual choir interface.
DATASET: The study involved the creation of a custom remote music recording of a choir performance specifically for the experiment.
APA FORMAT: Mróz, B., Odya, P., & Kostek, B. (2022). Creating a remote choir performance recording based on an ambisonic approach. Applied Sciences, 12(7), 3316. https://doi.org/10.3390/app12073316

AUTHOR: Sarkar, S., Thorpe, L., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M. B.
TOPIC: Leveraging Synthetic Data for Improving Chamber Ensemble Separation
METHODOLOGY: The authors propose a pre-training strategy and data augmentation pipeline using synthetic data to improve the performance of separation models on real-world recordings. They demonstrate that pre-training with synthetic ensembles significantly boosts the separation quality of vocal harmony mixtures.
DATASET: EnsembleSet (synthetic chamber ensemble dataset), URMP (real-world chamber music), Bach Chorales, and Barbershop Quartet datasets.
APA FORMAT: Sarkar, S., Thorpe, L., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M. B. (2023). Leveraging synthetic data for improving chamber ensemble separation. 2023 IEEE Workshop on Applications of Signal Processing to Audio and Acoustics (WASPAA), 1–5. https://doi.org/10.1109/WASPAA58344.2023.10248118

AUTHOR: Lanzendörfer, L. A., Pinkl, C., Grötschla, F., & Wattenhofer, R.
TOPIC: SepACap: Source Separation for A Cappella Music
METHODOLOGY: The authors introduced SepACap, an adaptation of the SepReformer architecture (a state-of-the-art speaker separation model). They enhanced the model with periodic activations (Snake functions) and a composite loss function designed to handle "silent stems"—ensuring the model doesn't hallucinate audio when a voice part is resting. They also used a power-set data augmentation strategy to train the model on varying ensemble sizes.
DATASET: JaCappella (a dataset of a cappella vocal ensembles) was used for training and evaluation, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in separating varying numbers of singers.
APA FORMAT: Lanzendörfer, L. A., Pinkl, C., Grötschla, F., & Wattenhofer, R. (2025). SepACap: Source separation for a cappella music. arXiv preprint arXiv:2509.26580. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.26580

AUTHOR: Boratto, T., Costa, G. d. O., Meireles, A., Alves, A. K. S. T. R., Saporetti, C. M., Bodini, M., Cury, A., & Goliatt, L.
TOPIC: Machine Learning with Evolutionary Parameter Tuning for Singing Registers Classification
METHODOLOGY: The study proposed a hybrid approach combining standard Machine Learning models (Support Vector Machines, Multi-Layer Perceptrons, and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB)) with a Differential Evolution (DE) algorithm. The DE algorithm automatically "evolves" and tunes the hyperparameters of the classifiers to maximize accuracy in identifying the physiological register of the singer from the audio signal.
DATASET: A custom dataset of 350 audio files encompassing chest, mixed, and head registers was constructed and processed using the TSFEL (Time Series Feature Extraction Library) to generate inputs for the models.
APA FORMAT: Boratto, T., Costa, G. d. O., Meireles, A., Alves, A. K. S. T. R., Saporetti, C. M., Bodini, M., Cury, A., & Goliatt, L. (2025). Machine learning with evolutionary parameter tuning for singing registers classification. Signals, 6(1), 9. https://doi.org/10.3390/signals6010009

AUTHOR: Weiss, C., Schreiber, H., & Müller, M.
TOPIC: Comparing Deep Models and Evaluation Strategies for Multi-Pitch Estimation in Music Recordings
METHODOLOGY: The authors conducted a rigorous comparative analysis of state-of-the-art deep learning architectures (including DeepSalience and U-Net variants) specifically applied to choral music. They investigated different output representations and training strategies to optimize the detection of individual voices within a choir section (e.g., distinguishing multiple tenors).
DATASET: The study utilized the Carus Audio Corpus (a large dataset of multitrack choral and sacred music) and the Schubert Winterreise dataset for evaluation.
APA FORMAT: Weiss, C., Schreiber, H., & Müller, M. (2022). Comparing deep models and evaluation strategies for multi-pitch estimation in music recordings. Transactions of the International Society for Music Information Retrieval (TISMIR), 5(1), 1–16. https://ieeexplore-ieee-org.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/document/9865174

AUTHOR: Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E.
TOPIC: Voice Assignment in Vocal Quartets Using Deep Learning Models Based on Pitch Salience
METHODOLOGY: The authors proposed a deep learning framework that takes a pitch salience representation (a time-frequency representation that emphasizes pitch content) as input. They trained Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to learn the specific tessituras and spectral characteristics of each voice part, allowing the model to assign detected pitches to the correct singer (SATB) even when their ranges overlap.
DATASET: The models were trained and evaluated using a combination of Bach Chorales (Western classical tradition) and Barbershop Quartets (a genre characterized by close harmony and specific tuning), testing the model's ability to generalize across different polyphonic vocal styles.
APA FORMAT: Cuesta, H., & Gómez, E. (2022). Voice assignment in vocal quartets using deep learning models based on pitch salience. Transactions of the International Society for Music Information Retrieval, 5(1), 99–112. https://go-gale-com.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/ps/i.do?p=AONE&u=dlsu&id=GALE%7CA705711774&v=2.1&it=r

AUTHOR: Yeh, Y.-C., Hsiao, W.-Y., Fukayama, S., Kitahara, T., Genchel, B., Liu, H.-M., Dong, H.-W., Chen, Y., Leong, T., & Yang, Y.-H.
TOPIC: Automatic Melody Harmonization with Triad Chords: A Comparative Study
METHODOLOGY:
The authors implemented and evaluated five distinct "canonical" models to harmonize melodies:

Template Matching (based on pitch class profiles)

Hidden Markov Model (HMM)

Genetic Algorithm (GA)

Deep Learning Models (specifically Bi-directional LSTM networks, including a proposed "MTHarmonizer" that predicts chords for half-bars). They compared these methods using both objective metrics (chord diversity, tonal distance) and subjective listening tests.
DATASET: A newly collected dataset of 9,226 melody/chord pairs (Lead sheets) was created specifically for this study to ensure a standardized training and testing ground for the different algorithms.
APA FORMAT: Yeh, Y.-C., Hsiao, W.-Y., Fukayama, S., Kitahara, T., Genchel, B., Liu, H.-M., Dong, H.-W., Chen, Y., Leong, T., & Yang, Y.-H. (2021). Automatic melody harmonization with triad chords: A comparative study. Journal of New Music Research, 50(1), 37–51. https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09298215.2021.1873392

AUTHOR: Cuesta, H., Kroher, N., Pikrakis, A., & Djordjevic, S.
TOPIC: DAACI-VoDAn: Improving Vocal Detection with New Data and Methods
METHODOLOGY: The authors proposed a data-driven framework called DAACI-VoDAn. They improved standard Deep Learning vocal detection models by introducing new data augmentation strategies that mimic the sparse and overlapping nature of choral textures. They evaluated the system's ability to reduce "false positives" (detecting a voice when it is actually resting) in dense mixtures.
DATASET: The study utilized a new proprietary dataset (DAACI-VoDAn dataset) alongside public corpora to benchmark the improvement in detection accuracy against standard baselines.
APA FORMAT: Cuesta, H., Kroher, N., Pikrakis, A., & Djordjevic, S. (2023). DAACI-VoDAn: Improving vocal detection with new data and methods. Proceedings of the 31st European Signal Processing Conference (EUSIPCO), 66–70. https://ieeexplore-ieee-org.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/document/10289758

AUTHOR: Rosenzweig, S., Cuesta, H., & Müller, M.
TOPIC: Adaptive Pitch-Shifting with Applications to Intonation Adjustment in a Cappella Recordings
METHODOLOGY: The researchers developed an adaptive pitch-shifting algorithm based on non-linear time-scale modification (TSM) and resampling. Unlike standard "Auto-Tune" which snaps pitch to a rigid grid, this algorithm calculates a time-varying resampling curve that respects the continuous nature of the singing voice, allowing for subtle intonation corrections that maintain the "choir effect."
DATASET: The method was evaluated using multitrack a cappella recordings (including segments from the Carus Audio Corpus) to verify that individual stems could be tuned effectively without bleeding artifacts.
APA FORMAT: Rosenzweig, S., Cuesta, H., & Müller, M. (2022). Adaptive pitch-shifting with applications to intonation adjustment in a cappella recordings. Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Digital Audio Effects (DAFx), 1–8. https://ieeexplore-ieee-org.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/document/9768268

AUTHOR: Hyodo, H., Takamichi, S., Nakamura, T., Koguchi, J., & Saruwatari, H.
TOPIC: DNN-Based Ensemble Singing Voice Synthesis with Interactions Between Singers
METHODOLOGY:
The authors developed a novel interaction-aware DNN framework.

Network Architecture: A multi-track structure where each voice part's generation is conditioned not just on its own score, but also on the scores and acoustic features of other voice parts.

Loss Functions: They introduced specific "interaction losses"—an F0 difference loss (to encourage pitch coherence between singers) and a Power difference loss (to balance volume levels), forcing the model to learn how singers adjust to one another.

Synchrony-Aware Padding: A data preprocessing technique ensuring that notes with simultaneous onsets across different parts are processed together, preserving musical timing integrity.
DATASET: The model was trained and evaluated using a dataset of quartet singing (likely a custom or proprietary dataset given the authors, but potentially based on the CSD or similar multitrack resources converted for SVS tasks), specifically focusing on scenarios with one singer per voice part (SATB-style).
APA FORMAT: Hyodo, H., Takamichi, S., Nakamura, T., Koguchi, J., & Saruwatari, H. (2024). DNN-based ensemble singing voice synthesis with interactions between singers. Proceedings of the 2024 IEEE Spoken Language Technology Workshop (SLT), 660–667. https://ieeexplore-ieee-org.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/document/10832340

AUTHOR: Weiss, C., & Müller, M.
TOPIC: Style Evolution in Western Choral Music: A Corpus-Based Strategy
METHODOLOGY: The authors developed a method to generate "evolution curves" for choral music features. They utilized a deep learning-based pitch-class estimation model (trained on aligned score-audio pairs) to robustly analyze the dense polyphony of choir recordings (from the Carus Audio Corpus). This approach allows for the automated analysis of harmony and tonality in actual choral performances, handling the acoustic complexity of choir reverberation and blend.
DATASET: Carus Audio Corpus (over 5,700 tracks of Western choral and sacred music).
APA FORMAT: Weiss, C., & Müller, M. (2024). Style evolution in Western choral music: A corpus-based strategy. Computational Humanities Research. Advance online publication. https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/aop-cambridge-core/content/view/18A4D5A8C065709EE3A84E75A5BA6D19/S297781582510016Xa.pdf/style-evolution-in-western-choral-music-a-corpus-based-strategy.pdf

AUTHOR: Kim, M., Choi, J., Kim, H., & Lee, K.
TOPIC: MuSE-SVS: Multi-Singer Emotional Singing Voice Synthesizer that Controls Emotional Intensity
METHODOLOGY: The researchers developed a multi-speaker Text-to-Speech (TTS) architecture adapted for singing. They introduced a statistical pitch predictor and a context-aware residual duration predictor to manage the subtle pitch and timing variations that occur when different singers perform together with varying emotional intensity. This allows for the creation of synthetic ensembles where each "singer" has a distinct but controllable character.
DATASET: Trained on a multi-singer dataset (likely including emotionally annotated singing samples) to learn the mapping between singer identity, emotion, and acoustic output.
APA FORMAT: Kim, M., Choi, J., Kim, H., & Lee, K. (2023). MuSE-SVS: Multi-singer emotional singing voice synthesizer that controls emotional intensity. IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 31, 1–15. https://ieeexplore-ieee-org.dlsu.idm.oclc.org/document/10179970

AUTHOR: Kadiri, S. R., Alku, P., & Yegnanarayana, B.
TOPIC: Analysis and classification of phonation types in speech and singing voice
METHODOLOGY:
- Acoustic analysis of phonation types (breathy, modal, pressed) in speech and singing
- Feature extraction without source–filter decomposition
- Signal processing methods: Zero Frequency Filtering (ZFF), Zero Time Windowing (ZTW), and Single Frequency Filtering (SFF)
- Extraction of scalar excitation features and cepstral coefficients (ZTWCCs, SFFCCs)
- Statistical analysis using one-way ANOVA and Tukey’s HSD
- Classification using Support Vector Machines (SVM) with 10-fold cross-validation
DATASET:
Speech dataset: 792 Finnish vowel samples from 11 speakers (breathy, modal, pressed phonation)

Singing dataset: 763 sustained vowels sung by a professional soprano (breathy, modal, pressed)

Both datasets downsampled to 8 kHz for analysis
APA FORMAT: Kadiri, S. R., Alku, P., & Yegnanarayana, B. (2020). Analysis and classification of phonation types in speech and singing voice. Speech Communication, 118, 33–47. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.specom.2020.02.004

AUTHOR: Faghih, B., Shoari Nejad, A., & Timoney, J.
TOPIC: Modelling note’s pitch and duration in trained professional singers
METHODOLOGY:
- Statistical modeling of singing performance deviations at the note level
- Bayesian Hierarchical Linear Regression (BHLR) to model pitch (F0) and duration deviations from score-based ground truth
- Analysis of eight explanatory variables, including pitch interval context, rests, note repetition (pitch drift), score-based duration, MIDI pitch code, and singing technique
- Separation of population-level and singer-specific effects through hierarchical modeling
- Model validation using posterior predictive checks and uncertainty calibration
- Derivation of empirical prediction ranges to estimate acceptable pitch and duration variability
DATASET:
- Annotated-VocalSet (derived from VocalSet)
- 2,688 solo singing recordings
- 20 trained professional singers (11 male, 9 female; multiple voice types)
- Approximately 7 hours of annotated singing data
- Includes scales, arpeggios, long tones, and musical excerpts
- Annotated with note onsets/offsets, MIDI pitch codes, singing techniques, and score-aligned metadata
APA FORMAT: Faghih, B., Shoari Nejad, A., & Timoney, J. (2024). Modelling note’s pitch and duration in trained professional singers. EURASIP Journal on Audio, Speech, and Music Processing, 2024(60). https://doi.org/10.1186/s13636-024-00380-4

AUTHOR: Zhang, X., Yu, Y., Gao, Y., Chen, X., & Li, W.
TOPIC: Research on Singing Voice Detection Based on a Long-Term Recurrent Convolutional Network with Vocal Separation and Temporal Smoothing
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a complete singing voice detection system that combines deep learning with signal-processing-based preprocessing and postprocessing. First, a U-Net–based singing voice separation model is applied to reduce accompaniment interference and isolate vocal signals. From the separated audio, multiple acoustic features (MFCC, PLP, LPCC, and spectral statistics) are extracted and fused. A Long-Term Recurrent Convolutional Network (LRCN), combining convolutional layers for feature fusion and LSTM layers for temporal context modeling, is then trained to classify vocal and non-vocal segments. Finally, temporal smoothing techniques (median filtering, HMM, and CRF) are applied to stabilize frame-level predictions and improve segment consistency.
DATASET:
- Jamendo Corpus (93 songs with vocal/non-vocal annotations)
- RWC Popular Music Dataset (100 annotated songs)
- MIR-1K (1000 vocal–accompaniment clips)
- iKala (252 professional singing excerpts)
- MedleyDB (61 multitrack recordings with derived vocal labels)
APA FORMAT: Zhang, X., Yu, Y., Gao, Y., Chen, X., & Li, W. (2020). Research on singing voice detection based on a long-term recurrent convolutional network with vocal separation and temporal smoothing. Electronics, 9(9), 1458. https://doi.org/10.3390/electronics9091458

AUTHOR: Li, X., & He, C.
TOPIC: Incorporating Cumulative Mean Normalized Difference Function towards Intepretable Monophonic Singing Voice Pitch Extraction
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a hybrid pitch extraction model, called YinNet, that integrates traditional digital signal processing (DSP) knowledge with deep learning for improved interpretability and robustness. The model embeds the cumulative mean normalized difference function (CMNDF) from the classical YIN algorithm into a neural network module to generate structured pitch candidates from raw audio waveforms. A Transformer-based multi-head self-attention (MHSA) decoder is then used to model inter-frame temporal dependencies and select the most reliable pitch trajectory. By combining DSP priors with neural representation learning, the system achieves accurate pitch estimation with fewer parameters while maintaining robustness to noise and improved generalization across datasets.
DATASET:
MIR-1K: 1,000 monophonic singing clips recorded from Chinese karaoke, with separated vocal tracks

iKala: 252 professionally recorded English singing excerpts

Both datasets evaluated using the isolated singing voice channel

Additional experiments conducted under varying noise conditions and cross-dataset testing
APA FORMAT: Li, X., & He, C. (2024). Incorporating cumulative mean normalized difference function towards interpretable monophonic singing voice pitch extraction. In Proceedings of the 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP) (pp. 709–713). IEEE. https://doi.org/10.1109/ICSP62122.2024.10743813

AUTHOR: Saxena, K. R., & Arora, V.
TOPIC: Interactive Singing Melody Extraction Based on Active Adaptation
METHODOLOGY: An interactive melody extraction framework using a CNN-based pitch classifier combined with confidence modeling to identify low-confidence frames for annotation. The system applies active learning to select informative samples and adapts to new musical domains using model-agnostic meta-learning, explicitly handling class imbalance to enable robust melody extraction with minimal annotated data.
DATASET:
MIR-1K (source domain)

ADC2004 and MIREX05 (polyphonic melody benchmarks)

HAR dataset: 523 Hindustani classical singing recordings (≈6.8 hours), publicly released
APA FORMAT: Saxena, K. R., & Arora, V. (2024). Interactive singing melody extraction based on active adaptation. IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 32, 2729–2742. https://doi.org/10.1109/TASLP.2024.3399614

AUTHOR: Zhao, Z., Luo, M., Qiao, X., Shao, C., & Sun, R.
TOPIC: Attention-Driven Time-Domain Convolutional Network for Source Separation of Vocal and Accompaniment
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed an end-to-end time-domain source separation model that operates directly on raw audio waveforms. The system is built on a temporal convolutional network (TCN) backbone and integrates two attention mechanisms: an Embedding Attention Module (EAM) to adaptively weight source embeddings during feature fusion, and an Efficient Convolutional Block Attention Module (E-CBAM) to enhance temporal and channel-wise feature representation. By combining attention-driven feature refinement with time-domain convolution, the model preserves phase information and improves separation quality. The network is trained using scale-invariant signal-to-noise ratio (SI-SNR) as the optimization objective and evaluated against state-of-the-art separation models.
DATASET:
MUSDB18: Standard music source separation benchmark with professionally mixed tracks

iKala: Singing voice separation dataset with isolated vocal and accompaniment tracks

Audio sampled at 44.1 kHz and evaluated under single-channel mixture conditions
APA FORMAT: Zhao, Z., Luo, M., Qiao, X., Shao, C., & Sun, R. (2025). Attention-driven time-domain convolutional network for source separation of vocal and accompaniment. Electronics, 14(20), 3982. https://doi.org/10.3390/electronics14203982

AUTHOR: Parekh, S., Essid, S., & Richard, G.
TOPIC: Audiovisual singing voice separation
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a multimodal singing voice separation system that jointly exploits audio and visual information from performance videos. The model uses convolutional neural networks to extract features from audio spectrograms and synchronized video frames capturing mouth and facial movements. These modality-specific representations are fused within a deep neural architecture to guide vocal–accompaniment separation, allowing the system to better distinguish the target singing voice from instruments and backing vocals. The approach explicitly leverages visual cues related to vocal activity to improve separation robustness in challenging polyphonic and visually diverse performance conditions. Objective separation metrics and subjective listening tests are used to validate the effectiveness of audiovisual fusion over audio-only baselines.
DATASET:
URSing dataset (introduced in this study)
- Audiovisual recordings of solo singing performances
- Includes professional and semi-professional singers
- Contains challenging scenarios with backing vocals and accompaniment

Additional evaluation conducted on standard audio-only singing voice separation benchmarks
APA FORMAT: Parekh, S., Essid, S., & Richard, G. (2021). Audiovisual singing voice separation. Transactions of the International Society for Music Information Retrieval, 4(1), 246–259. https://doi.org/10.5334/tismir.86

AUTHOR: Faghih, B., Chakraborty, S., Yaseen, A., & Timoney, J.
TOPIC: A New Method for Detecting Onset and Offset for Singing in Real-Time and Offline Environments
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a rule-based and signal-processing-driven method for detecting note onsets, offsets, and transitions in solo singing performances. The approach analyzes fundamental frequency (F0) contours and pitch trajectory slopes to identify temporal boundaries between notes, explicitly accounting for singing-specific characteristics such as legato transitions, soft onsets, and vibrato. Statistical decision rules are applied to distinguish true note boundaries from expressive pitch fluctuations. The system is designed to operate in both real-time and offline settings and is evaluated against multiple state-of-the-art onset detection algorithms.
DATASET:
Annotated-VocalSet (derived from VocalSet): professionally recorded solo singing data

Includes multiple voice types and singing techniques

Supplementary evaluation conducted on additional solo singing recordings to test real-time performance and robustness
APA FORMAT: Faghih, B., Chakraborty, S., Yaseen, A., & Timoney, J. (2022). A new method for detecting onset and offset for singing in real-time and offline environments. Applied Sciences, 12(9), 4487. https://doi.org/10.3390/app12094487

AUTHOR: He, Z., & Feng, Y.
TOPIC: Singing transcription from polyphonic music using melody contour filtering
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a signal-processing-based singing transcription system that extracts the predominant vocal melody from polyphonic music and converts it into note-level transcriptions. The approach constructs a salience spectrogram to emphasize vocal pitch candidates, applies edge-searching techniques to track continuous melody contours, and uses contour filtering with sliding-window breakpoint detection to remove unstable or spurious segments. Stable pitch lines are then segmented into notes by detecting onset and offset boundaries, yielding pitch and duration estimates for singing transcription. The method emphasizes interpretability and robustness to accompaniment interference without relying on deep learning.
DATASET:
MIR-1K: Karaoke-style polyphonic music with isolated vocal references

iKala: Professionally recorded singing excerpts with accompaniment

ADC2004 and MIREX melody extraction datasets for benchmarking

Evaluation conducted using standard MIREX melody and transcription metrics
APA FORMAT: He, Z., & Feng, Y. (2021). Singing transcription from polyphonic music using melody contour filtering. Applied Sciences, 11(9), 4051. https://doi.org/10.3390/app11094051

AUTHOR: Wager, S., Tzanetakis, G., Wang, C.-i., & Kim, M.
TOPIC: Deep Autotuner: A Pitch Correcting Network for Singing Performances
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a data-driven, score-free pitch correction system that predicts note-level pitch shifts for solo singing based on harmonic alignment with the accompaniment. The method operates on constant-Q transform (CQT) representations of both the vocal and backing tracks and uses a deep neural network composed of convolutional layers followed by a gated recurrent unit (GRU) to model note-level temporal context. Instead of snapping pitches to discrete scale degrees, the model estimates continuous pitch shifts that correct unintended intonation errors while preserving expressive variations such as vibrato and pitch bending. Training data are generated by synthetically detuning in-tune performances, allowing the network to learn to distinguish intentional expression from pitch inaccuracies. The system is evaluated using both objective pitch error metrics and subjective listening tests.
DATASET:
Intonation dataset (derived from Smule recordings)

4,702 solo karaoke-style singing performances selected for good intonation

Western popular music repertoire with aligned accompaniment tracks

Split into training (4,561), validation (49), and test (64) performances

Additional real-world test set with 24 newly recorded singing performances for subjective evaluation
APA FORMAT: Wager, S., Tzanetakis, G., Wang, C.-i., & Kim, M. (2020). Deep autotuner: A pitch correcting network for singing performances. In Proceedings of the IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP) (pp. 246–250). IEEE. https://doi.org/10.1109/ICASSP40776.2020.9053208

AUTHOR: Parker, J. D., Spijkervet, J., Kosta, K., Yesiler, F., Kuznetsov, B., Wang, J., Avent, M., Chen, J., & Le, D.
TOPIC: Generative Audio / Stem Generation
METHODOLOGY:
- "Listening" Model: Uses a non-autoregressive Transformer that conditions on musical context (e.g., drums) to generate coherent stems (e.g., bass).

- Contextual Coherence: Optimizes for spectral and rhythmic alignment with the input mix rather than just audio quality.
DATASET: - Proprietary & Open Source: Trained on a combination of internal datasets and open multi-track data to learn stem relationships.
APA FORMAT: Parker, J. D., Spijkervet, J., Kosta, K., Yesiler, F., Kuznetsov, B., Wang, J., Avent, M., Chen, J., & Le, D. (2024). StemGen: A music generation model that listens. In 2024 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP) (pp. 1116-1120). IEEE.

AUTHOR: Weck, B., Manco, I., Benetos, E., Quinton, E., Fazekas, G., & Bogdanov, D.
TOPIC: Music Understanding / Evaluation
METHODOLOGY:
- Benchmark Creation: Developed MuChoMusic, a benchmark to test if multimodal "Audio LLMs" genuinely understand music or rely on text priors.

- Evaluation: Tested 5 open-source models on 1,187 validated questions, revealing a significant "text bias" where models ignore audio cues.
DATASET: - MuChoMusic: 1,187 manually validated multiple-choice questions based on tracks from MTG-Jamendo and other open corpora.
APA FORMAT: Weck, B., Manco, I., Benetos, E., Quinton, E., Fazekas, G., & Bogdanov, D. (2024). MuChoMusic: Evaluating music understanding in multimodal audio-language models. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 825-833).

AUTHOR: Steinmetz, C. J., Singh, S., Comunità, M., Ibnyahya, I., Yuan, S., Benetos, E., & Reiss, J. D
TOPIC: Differentiable Audio Effects / Style Transfer
METHODOLOGY:
- Inference-Time Optimization (ITO): Optimizes the parameters of a differentiable DSP chain (EQ, Compressor) to match a reference style at inference time.

- White-Box AI: Produces interpretable plugin parameters rather than a black-box audio output.
DATASET: - Multiple: MTG-Jamendo, ENST-Drums, FSD50k, and GuitarSet used to train the style-matching encoder.
APA FORMAT: Steinmetz, C. J., Singh, S., Comunità, M., Ibnyahya, I., Yuan, S., Benetos, E., & Reiss, J. D. (2024). ST-ITO: Controlling audio effects for style transfer with inference-time optimization. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 661-668).

AUTHOR: Wen, Y., Kim, M., & Smaragdis, P.
TOPIC: Interactive Source Separation
METHODOLOGY:
- User-Guided Diffusion: Integrates user inputs (humming, text descriptions) into a diffusion model to guide the separation of specific sources.

- Guided Priors: Uses a diffusion model conditioned on the user's "query" to isolate targets not defined in standard stems.
DATASET: - MoisesDB & HumTrans: Used to train the model on complex mixtures and align humming prompts with stems.
APA FORMAT: Wen, Y., Kim, M., & Smaragdis, P. (2025). User-guided generative source separation. In Proceedings of the 26th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 821-829).

AUTHOR: Afchar, D., Meseguer-Brocal, G., Akesbi, K., & Hennequin, R.
TOPIC: Deepfake Detection / AI Artifacts
METHODOLOGY:
- Fourier Analysis: Mathematically proves that generative architectures (specifically deconvolution layers) leave distinct spectral "fingerprints."

- Detection: Proposes a simple, interpretable criterion for detecting AI-generated music based on these spectral periodicities.
DATASET: - Synthetic Data: Generated outputs from commercial models (Suno, Udio) and open-source models (MusicGen) analyzed against real audio.
APA FORMAT: Afchar, D., Meseguer-Brocal, G., Akesbi, K., & Hennequin, R. (2025). A Fourier explanation of AI-music artifacts. In Proceedings of the 26th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 739-746).

AUTHOR: Riou, A., Lattner, S., Hadjeres, G., Anslow, M., & Peeters, G.
TOPIC: Stem Compatibility / Retrieval
METHODOLOGY:
- Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA): Learns a shared embedding space where compatible stems (e.g., bass and drums) map closely together.

- Self-Supervised Learning: Predicts the embedding of a missing stem from the mix context, enabling "zero-shot" compatibility search.
DATASET: - Multi-track Dataset: Large-scale internal dataset of separated tracks used to learn musical compatibility.
APA FORMAT: Riou, A., Lattner, S., Hadjeres, G., Anslow, M., & Peeters, G. (2024). Stem-JEPA: A joint-embedding predictive architecture for musical stem compatibility estimation. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 625-633).

AUTHOR: Take, O., Watanabe, K., Nakatsuka, T., Cheng, T., Nakano, T., Goto, M., Takamichi, S., & Saruwatari, H.
TOPIC: Audio Effects / Reverse Engineering
METHODOLOGY:
- Iterative Estimation: A deep neural network iteratively estimates the last applied effect in a chain and "undoes" it to recover the dry signal.

- Blind Estimation: Capable of reconstructing the effect chain without prior knowledge of the specific plugins used.
DATASET: - Simulated FX Chains: Guitar recordings processed with various chains of distortion, EQ, and reverb for training.
APA FORMAT: Take, O., Watanabe, K., Nakatsuka, T., Cheng, T., Nakano, T., Goto, M., Takamichi, S., & Saruwatari, H. (2024). Audio effect chain estimation and dry signal recovery from multi-effect-processed musical signals. In Proceedings of the 27th International Conference on Digital Audio Effects (DAFx) (pp. 1-8).

AUTHOR: Manco, I., Salamon, J., & Nieto, O.
TOPIC: Cross-Modal Retrieval / Contrastive Learning
METHODOLOGY:
- Augmentation Recipe: Uses LLMs to rewrite captions ("Augment"), drops semantic parts ("Drop"), and swaps entities ("Swap") to create hard negatives.

- Efficiency: Improves retrieval performance and robustness without increasing the dataset size.
DATASET: - MSD & MTG-Jamendo: Standard datasets enriched with LLM-generated synthetic captions for contrastive learning.
APA FORMAT: Manco, I., Salamon, J., & Nieto, O. (2024). Augment, drop & swap: Improving diversity in LLM captions for efficient music-text representation learning. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 938-945).

AUTHOR: Plaja-Roglans, G., Serra, X., & Rocamora, M.
TOPIC: Non-Western Source Separation
METHODOLOGY:
- Regression-Guided Diffusion: Uses a regression model to estimate "bleeding" and guide a latent diffusion model for cleaner separation.

- Context: Specifically targets Carnatic music, handling non-Western pitch curves (gamakas) and dense instrumentation.
DATASET: - Saraga & Sanidha: High-quality multitrack datasets specifically curated for Carnatic music research.
APA FORMAT: Plaja-Roglans, G., Serra, X., & Rocamora, M. (2025). Singing voice separation from Carnatic music mixtures using a regression-guided latent diffusion model. In Proceedings of the 26th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 830-838).

AUTHOR: Riley, X., Guo, Z., Edwards, A. C., & Dixon, S.
TOPIC: Automatic Transcription / Dataset
METHODOLOGY:
- Dataset Creation: Built the largest aligned dataset for classical guitar using Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) and fine-grained alignment.

- Benchmarking: Established new baselines for polyphonic guitar transcription using the new data.
DATASET: - GAPS: 14 hours of aligned audio and scores from over 200 performers (Guitar-Aligned Performance Scores).
APA FORMAT: Riley, X., Guo, Z., Edwards, A. C., & Dixon, S. (2024). GAPS: A large and diverse classical guitar dataset and benchmark transcription model. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 611-617).

AUTHOR: Doh, S., Lee, M., Jeong, D., & Nam, J.
TOPIC: Text-to-Music Retrieval
METHODOLOGY:
- TTMR++: Uses a fine-tuned LLM to enrich sparse music tags into dense, descriptive captions.

- Joint Embedding: Trains a retrieval model on these enriched captions to handle complex natural language queries more effectively.
DATASET: - Music Tag Datasets: Existing tag datasets augmented with synthetic descriptions generated by the LLM.
APA FORMAT: Doh, S., Lee, M., Jeong, D., & Nam, J. (2024). Enriching music descriptions with a finetuned-LLM and metadata for text-to-music retrieval. In 2024 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP) (pp. 826-830). IEEE.

AUTHOR: Morsi, A., Zhang, H., Maezawa, A., Dixon, S., & Serra, X.
TOPIC: Performance Analysis / Education
METHODOLOGY:
- Mistake Taxonomy: Developed a hierarchical classification of common piano student errors.

- Simulation Pipeline: Created a system to inject realistic mistakes into MIDI performances to train assessment models.
DATASET: - Piano Performance Data: Real-world student recordings used to model error distributions and create synthetic training data.
APA FORMAT: Morsi, A., Zhang, H., Maezawa, A., Dixon, S., & Serra, X. (2024). Simulating piano performance mistakes for music learning contexts. In Proceedings of the 18th Sound and Music Computing Conference (SMC).

AUTHOR: Novack, Z., McAuley, J., Berg-Kirkpatrick, T., & Bryan, N. J.
TOPIC: Generative Music / Optimization
METHODOLOGY:
- T-Optimization: Optimized the reverse diffusion trajectory to reduce sampling steps without quality loss.

- Distillation: Distilled a large pre-trained diffusion model for real-time inference capabilities.
DATASET: - MusicCaps: Used for evaluation of text-adherence and audio quality (FAD/CLAP scores).
APA FORMAT: Novack, Z., McAuley, J., Berg-Kirkpatrick, T., & Bryan, N. J. (2024). DITTO-2: Distilled diffusion inference-time T-optimization for music generation. In Proceedings of the 25th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 874-881).

AUTHOR: Sarkar, S., Moomijan, V., Woods, B., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M.
TOPIC: Source Separation Evaluation
METHODOLOGY:
- Segment-wise Evaluation: Critiques standard averaged metrics (SDR) which hide local failures.

- New Metrics: Proposes perceptual metrics that better correlate with human judgment of separation quality.
DATASET: - MusDB18: Used as the test bed to demonstrate the flaws of traditional evaluation metrics.
APA FORMAT: Sarkar, S., Moomijan, V., Woods, B., Benetos, E., & Sandler, M. (2025). Looking beyond averaged metrics in music source separation. In Proceedings of the 26th International Society for Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR) (pp. 839-846).

AUTHOR: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., Petermann, D., & Gómez, E.
TOPIC: A Deep-Learning Based Framework for Source Separation, Analysis, and Synthesis of Choral Ensembles
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a comprehensive deep learning framework for modeling choral ensembles that integrates source separation, pitch analysis, and singing synthesis. Multiple end-to-end neural architectures (U-Net, Wave-U-Net, Open-Unmix, and Conv-TasNet) are adapted and evaluated for SATB voice separation in unison and multi-singer choir recordings. The framework further introduces a method for estimating perceived unison pitch from multiple singers and supports analysis–resynthesis pipelines, enabling tasks such as unison-to-solo conversion, solo-to-unison synthesis, and choral remixing. Both objective metrics and listening tests are used to assess system performance and perceptual quality.
DATASET:
ChoralSingingDataset (SATB choir recordings with multiple singers per part)

Cantoría dataset (professional choir recordings)

Bach Chorales (synthetic and score-informed choral data for controlled experiments)

Includes real and synthetic choral audio covering unison and polyphonic SATB textures
APA FORMAT: Chandna, P., Cuesta, H., Petermann, D., & Gómez, E. (2022). A deep-learning based framework for source separation, analysis, and synthesis of choral ensembles. Frontiers in Signal Processing, 2, 808594. https://doi.org/10.3389/frsip.2022.808594

AUTHOR: Yuan, W., Wang, S., Wang, J., Unoki, M., & Wang, W.
TOPIC: Unsupervised Deep Unfolded Representation Learning for Singing Voice Separation.
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed an unsupervised singing voice separation framework based on deep unfolded representation learning, where each network layer corresponds to an iteration of an optimization algorithm. The model, referred to as DURL, explicitly incorporates signal priors such as non-negativity, sparsity, and reconstruction constraints into a learnable encoder–decoder architecture. An extended variant integrates optimal transport (OT) to better align latent representations of vocal and accompaniment sources. By embedding optimization principles into the network design, the approach achieves interpretable latent representations while maintaining competitive separation performance without requiring labeled source data. The system is evaluated under both unsupervised and supervised settings and compared against conventional deep encoders and attention-based models.
DATASET:
MUSDB18: Standard music source separation benchmark
- 150 professionally mixed tracks
- Includes isolated vocal and accompaniment stems

Experiments conducted on vocal–accompaniment mixtures derived from the dataset

Evaluation performed using SI-SDR and related source separation metrics
APA FORMAT: Yuan, W., Wang, S., Wang, J., Unoki, M., & Wang, W. (2023). Unsupervised deep unfolded representation learning for singing voice separation. IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 31, 2503–2517. https://doi.org/10.1109/TASLP.2023.3290791

AUTHOR: Bhattarai, B., Pandeya, Y. R., Jie, Y., Lamichhane, A. K., & Lee, J.
TOPIC: High-Resolution Representation Learning and Recurrent Neural Network for Singing Voice Separation
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed a deep learning–based singing voice separation system that preserves fine-grained spectral details by maintaining high-resolution feature representations throughout the network. The model adapts a High-Resolution Network (HRNet) architecture, originally developed for computer vision, to time–frequency audio representations and integrates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) layers to capture temporal dependencies in singing performances. By avoiding aggressive downsampling, the system retains harmonic structure critical for vocal separation. The network predicts soft time–frequency masks that are applied to mixture spectrograms to separate vocal and accompaniment signals. Performance is evaluated against state-of-the-art separation models using standard MIR metrics.
DATASET:
MUSDB18: Standard benchmark for music source separation

iKala: Singing voice separation dataset with professional recordings

NISVS dataset (introduced in this study):
- 100 Korean Pansori singing recordings
- Includes culturally specific solo singing performances

All datasets evaluated under single-channel mixture conditions
APA FORMAT: Bhattarai, B., Pandeya, Y. R., Jie, Y., Lamichhane, A. K., & Lee, J. (2023). High-resolution representation learning and recurrent neural network for singing voice separation. Circuits, Systems, and Signal Processing, 42, 4800–4824. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00034-023-02377-9

AUTHOR: Park, J., Choi, K., Oh, S., Kim, L., & Park, J.
TOPIC: Note-level singing melody transcription with transformers
METHODOLOGY: The researchers proposed an end-to-end note-level singing melody transcription system based on a sequence-to-sequence Transformer architecture.
